2604.24608.title: Learning to Route Queries to Heads for Attention-based Re-ranking with Large Language Models
2604.24608.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently been explored as fine-grained zero-shot re-rankers by leveraging attention signals to estimate document relevance. However, existing methods either aggregate attention signals across all heads or rely on a statically selected subset identified by heuristic rules. This solution can be suboptimal because the informative heads can vary across queries or domains. Moreover, naively combining multiple heads can degrade performance due to redundancy or conflicting ranking signals. In this paper, we propose a query-dependent head selection method, RouteHead, for attention-based re-ranking with LLMs. Specifically, we learn a lightweight router that can map each query to an optimal head set, and relevance scores are computed by aggregating attention signals only from these heads. Since query-to-head optimal labels are unavailable, we first construct pseudo labels via an offline search. The router represents each head with a learnable embedding and represents each query using an embedding extracted from the hidden states of the frozen LLM. Then it is trained on the pseudo labels with a sparsity regularizer. Experiments on diverse benchmarks and multiple LLM backbones show that the proposed method consistently outperforms strong baselines.
2604.24608.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）最近被探索作為細粒度的零樣本重排序器，利用注意力信號來估計文件的相關性。<lb/>然而，現有的方法要麼在所有頭部之間聚合注意力信號，要麼依賴於由啟發式規則識別的靜態選擇子集。<lb/>這種解決方案可能不是最佳的，因為信息豐富的頭部在查詢或領域之間可能會有所不同。<lb/>此外，天真地結合多個頭部可能會因冗餘或衝突的排名信號而降低性能。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一種基於查詢的頭部選擇方法，RouteHead，用於基於注意力的重排序，配合LLMs。<lb/>具體而言，我們學習了一個輕量級的路由器，可以將每個查詢映射到最佳頭部集，並且相關性分數僅通過聚合來自這些頭部的注意力信號來計算。<lb/>由於查詢到頭部的最佳標籤不可用，我們首先通過離線搜索構建偽標籤。<lb/>路由器用可學習的嵌入表示每個頭部，並使用從凍結的LLM的隱藏狀態中提取的嵌入來表示每個查詢。<lb/>然後，它在偽標籤上進行訓練，並使用稀疏性正則化器。<lb/>在多樣的基準和多個LLM骨幹上的實驗表明，所提出的方法始終優於強基線。
2604.24608.author: Yuxing Tian et.al.
2604.24608.authors: Yuxing Tian, Fengran Mo, Zhiqi Huang, Weixu Zhang, Jian-Yun Nie
